extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam won the field sprint after Jumbo-Visma controlled the final climb, taking bonus seconds while KÜNG retained overall lead.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma paced final classified climb to ensure sprint finish despite having no sprinter, revealing protective GC strategy.
EVENEPOEL contested double intermediate sprint tiso kilometer for bonus seconds, signaling aggressive secondary objectives despite sprint stage profile.
Team DSM employed BARDET domestique role for sprint leadout, unusual GC rider deployment suggesting classification priorities.

rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: first win since February crash during classics, sprint form returning perfectly timed before Tour debut
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: actively contesting intermediate sprint bonus seconds on sprint stage, hunting marginal GC gains opportunistically
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: fifth place after altitude training block on final Tour de Suisse suggests reasonable sprint form maintenance
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: deployed as leadout domestique for DSM sprinter reveals diminished GC leadership status within team
